operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot selection view shows robot details for the selected robot
operational_knowledge_units.summary: In the Robot option view, selecting a robot is used to view that robot's details. The selected robot is visually indicated by a box around it.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'When Robot option is selected, select a robot to view robot details.'
Slide text: 'The robot selected will have a box around it showing its selected.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot Selection overview: when the Robot option is selected, choose a robot to view robot details. The selected robot is shown with a box around it.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Tote information displayed under robot ID indicates tote association and removal condition
operational_knowledge_units.summary: If a robot has a tote, the tote appears under the robot ID. A tote value prefixed with a question mark indicates the robot has been removed but the tote has not. If the robot does not have a tote, the UI shows 'layer_1'.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot Id.'
Slide text: 'If the tote has a “?” before it, that means the robot has been removed but the tote has not.'
Slide text: 'If a robot doesn’t have a tote it will say layer_1'

relationships.to: robot removed but tote not removed

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot Selection tote display behavior: tote shows under the robot ID when present. A '?' before the tote means the robot has been removed but the tote has not. If no tote exists, the display says layer_1.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot colors communicate connection, task, idle, and fault or low battery states
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Robot color coding in the UI conveys robot status. Purple means disconnected, blue or flashing blue means in task/searching for label, green means not in a task, and red means fault or low battery.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'Blue/Flashing Blue: robot in task/searching for label'
Slide text: 'Green: robot not in a task'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot color meanings in Robot Selection: purple = disconnected, blue or flashing blue = in task/searching for label, green = not in a task, red = fault or low battery.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Select a robot to view robot details in Robot Selection
preconditions: Robot option is selected in the UI
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide-level Robot Selection overview describing selecting a robot and the box indicator for the selected robot.

steps.action: In the Robot option view, select a robot.
steps.expected_result: The system shows that robot's details.
steps.visual_region_hint: Robot list or robot display area in the Robot Selection view

steps.action: Confirm the robot is selected by checking for a box around it.
steps.expected_result: A box appears around the selected robot.
steps.visual_region_hint: Selected robot tile or robot graphic

procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Navigation attempt: in Robot Selection, select a robot to view its details and confirm selection by the box around the robot.
